hero.title: Welcome — We're Glad You Found Us
hero.subtitle: Everything you need to know before your first visit

firstVisitSteps.title: Arrive 15 Minutes Early
firstVisitSteps.description: This gives us time to review your paperwork and make you comfortable.

firstVisitSteps.title: Comprehensive Exam
firstVisitSteps.description: Digital X-rays, oral health assessment, and a conversation about your goals.

firstVisitSteps.title: Personalized Treatment Plan
firstVisitSteps.description: We'll explain our findings, discuss options, and create a plan that fits your needs and budget.

firstVisitSteps.title: Start Treatment or Schedule Next Steps
firstVisitSteps.description: For simple treatments, we can often start the same day.

whatToBring: Medical history or relevant health records
Referral letter from previous dentist (if applicable)

patientForms.title: New Patient Forms
patientForms.description: Save time by filling out your forms before your visit.

preparingChild.title: Bringing Your Child?
tips: Use positive language about the dentist — avoid words like 'hurt' or 'shot'
Read children's books about dental visits beforehand
Let them bring a favorite toy or stuffed animal for comfort
Schedule morning appointments when kids are usually at their best

faqs.question: How long will my first appointment take?
faqs.answer: Plan for about 60-90 minutes. This allows time for paperwork, a comprehensive exam, X-rays, and a discussion of your treatment plan.

faqs.question: Will I need to have any work done at the first visit?
faqs.answer: Usually the first visit is diagnostic only. If you have an urgent issue, we may be able to address it the same day.

faqs.question: Can I bring my family?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! We see patients of all ages and can often schedule family members back-to-back for convenience.
